question_text:
24 Game

You are given an integer array cards of length 4. You have four cards, each containing a number in the range [1, 9]. You should use the operators +, -, *, and / to evaluate a mathematical expression. You want to know if you can get the value 24.

  • Every card must be used exactly once.
  • You may only use each operator once.
  • Division is real division, not integer division. For example, 4 / (1 - 2/3) = 12.

Constraints:

  • cards.length == 4
  • 1 <= cards[i] <= 9
input_format: An integer array cards of exactly 4 integers, each between 1 and 9.
output_format: true if it is possible to compute 24 using the 4 numbers and arithmetic operations, false otherwise.
explanation:
Backtracking over all Permutations and Operations

Try all permutations of the 4 numbers and all combinations of 3 operators among +,-,*,/. Use backtracking: at each step pick two operands from the remaining list, apply one of 4 operations, and recurse with the resulting list of remaining numbers. Base case: if one number remains and equals 24 (within epsilon), return true.

Time: O(4! × 4^3) = constant. Space: O(1).
